title: Canon File
description: Validates the WHOLE canon.json file -- the frozen, hash-versioned, cross-segment name/realia glossary. entries{} holds resolved ACCEPTED canon-entry.schema.json-shaped records keyed by source_form; review_queue[] holds low-confidence/disputed candidates in the QUEUED shape from canon-batch.schema.json's discriminated union (disposition:"review_queue"); generation_hashes stamps the particle_config/derivation-bundle provenance canon.json was generated under, so select_segments.py's derivation-state gate can detect staleness. All three top-level keys are required unconditionally -- a merge that skipped either generation_hashes stamp, or that never initialized review_queue, must fail schema validation loudly rather than be discovered later mid-dispatch.

entries.description: Resolved, ACCEPTED canon entries keyed by source_form.

review_queue.description: Low-confidence/disputed candidates, each in the QUEUED shape from canon-batch.schema.json's discriminated union -- not yet resolved into entries{}.

generation_hashes.description: Provenance stamps recorded at merge time by the glossary-pass merge step -- NEVER manifest.json. particle_config_hash and derivation_bundle_hash together let select_segments.py's derivation-state gate detect an edit to the particle config or to bootstrap_names.py/segpack.py since this canon.json was last (re)generated.
